criteria.name: component_architecture_and_react_patterns
criteria.description: Is the component tree well-structured with clear separation of concerns? Are custom hooks used to encapsulate reusable logic (localStorage sync, drag-and-drop, undo, keyboard shortcuts)? Is state managed via useReducer + Context without prop drilling? Are components small, focused, and composable? Is memoization (React.memo, useMemo, useCallback) used where it prevents meaningful re-renders?
criteria.rubric: 90-100: Clean component hierarchy with Board > Column > Card decomposition; 4+ well-designed custom hooks encapsulating distinct concerns; useReducer + Context for global state with no prop drilling; appropriate memoization that prevents measurable re-renders; no God components. 60-80: Good component structure but one concern leaks across boundaries — e.g. drag logic mixed into Card component instead of a hook, or Context used but with prop drilling in some places, or memoization missing where it matters. 30-59: Components exist but are large and monolithic; state management is ad-hoc useState scattered across components; custom hooks absent or trivial wrappers. 0-29: Single-file application or components with no clear structure; state management is broken or absent.

criteria.name: typescript_strictness
criteria.description: Is TypeScript used in strict mode with no `any` types? Are shared types/interfaces well-defined for Card, Column, Board, Actions, etc.? Are discriminated unions used for action types in the reducer? Are generic types used where appropriate (e.g. useLocalStorage<T>)? Are event handler types correct (DragEvent, KeyboardEvent, etc.)?
criteria.rubric: 90-100: tsconfig strict mode enabled; zero `any` types; well-defined type hierarchy (Card, Column, Board, Action discriminated union); generic custom hooks; correct DOM event types throughout; type-safe reducer with exhaustive action matching. 60-80: Strict mode on and mostly well-typed, but a few `any` types or type assertions creep in, or action types are string literals instead of discriminated unions, or event handlers use generic Event instead of specific types. 30-59: Types exist but are loose — frequent `any`, no discriminated unions, interfaces are incomplete or overly permissive. 0-29: TypeScript used as JavaScript with types — pervasive `any`, no interfaces, no strict mode.

criteria.name: drag_and_drop_implementation
criteria.description: Is drag-and-drop implemented using the HTML5 Drag and Drop API directly (not a library)? Does it handle dragstart, dragover, dragenter, dragleave, drop, and dragend correctly? Is there clear visual feedback (ghost element, drop placeholder, column highlight)? Can cards be reordered within a column and moved between columns? Does it handle edge cases (dropping outside a column, rapid drag actions)?
criteria.rubric: 90-100: Full HTML5 DnD API implementation with all relevant events handled; visible drop placeholder showing insertion point; source card visually dimmed during drag; columns highlight on dragover; within-column reordering and cross-column moves both work; edge cases handled gracefully (no crash on drop outside, no duplicate cards). 60-80: DnD works for cross-column moves and within-column reorder but visual feedback is minimal (no placeholder or highlight), or one edge case causes a bug. 30-59: Basic DnD that moves cards between columns but no within-column reorder, or uses a library instead of the HTML5 API, or visual feedback is absent. 0-29: Drag-and-drop is not implemented or is non-functional.

criteria.name: accessibility_and_keyboard_support
criteria.description: Are all CRUD operations keyboard-accessible? Can cards be moved between columns via keyboard shortcuts (e.g. Alt+Arrow)? Are appropriate ARIA roles, labels, and live regions used? Is focus management correct after actions (card create, delete, move)? Does the dark mode toggle work and persist?
criteria.rubric: 90-100: All operations keyboard-accessible; Alt+Arrow (or equivalent documented shortcut) moves focused card between columns; ARIA roles on board/columns/cards; live regions announce drag results and filter changes; focus moves logically after create/delete/move; dark mode toggles and persists to localStorage; prefers-color-scheme respected as default. 60-80: Keyboard navigation works for most operations but card movement shortcuts missing or ARIA roles incomplete; dark mode present but missing persistence or system preference detection. 30-59: Basic tab navigation works but no keyboard shortcuts for card movement; ARIA roles absent; dark mode partial or broken. 0-29: Not keyboard accessible; no ARIA; no dark mode.

criteria.name: search_filter_and_undo
criteria.description: Does the search input filter cards in real-time by title and description? Do priority and tag filters work as multi-select? Are non-matching cards dimmed rather than removed? Does Ctrl+Z/Cmd+Z undo the last action? Is the undo stack maintained correctly across action types (move, delete, edit)?
criteria.rubric: 90-100: Real-time search across title and description; multi-select priority and tag filters; non-matching cards dimmed (not hidden) preserving layout; Ctrl+Z/Cmd+Z undoes last action; undo works for moves, deletes, edits, and column operations; history stack depth of 20+; undo state survives across filter changes. 60-80: Search and filters work but cards are hidden instead of dimmed, or undo works for some actions but not all (e.g. undoes deletes but not moves), or undo stack is shallow. 30-59: Search works but filters are missing or broken; undo is absent or only undoes the most recent action type. 0-29: No search, no filters, no undo.

criteria.name: code_quality_and_project_setup
criteria.description: Does the project run with `npm install && npm run dev`? Is Vite configured correctly with the React plugin? Is Tailwind CSS v4 set up properly? Are files organized as specified (types/, context/, hooks/, components/, utils/)? Is the code clean, readable, and free of dead code? Are animations smooth (CSS transitions or Framer Motion)?
criteria.rubric: 90-100: Project starts cleanly; Vite + React plugin + Tailwind all configured correctly; file structure matches spec; code is clean with consistent naming; CSS transitions on card/column operations are smooth; no dead code or commented-out blocks; package.json has correct scripts and minimal dependencies. 60-80: Project runs but one config is slightly off (e.g. Tailwind dark mode not configured in tailwind.config), or file structure deviates from spec, or animations are jerky/absent in some places. 30-59: Project runs with manual fixes needed; poor file organization; no animations; significant dead code or console warnings. 0-29: Project does not run; missing critical configuration; dependencies not listed in package.json.
